message:
fix(refinery): use commit SHA instead of branch name for MR dedup (gt-8qw)

MR deduplication compared branch names to detect duplicate submissions.
When a polecat pushed new commits to the same branch after a gate failure,
the dedup found the old MR and reused it — the refinery then rejected the
re-submission as already processed.

Fix: add commit_sha field to MR descriptions and compare both branch name
AND commit SHA for dedup. Same branch + different SHA = new submission,
which creates a fresh MR and supersedes the old one.

Changes:
- Add CommitSHA field to MRFields (fields.go)
- Add FindMRForBranchAndSHA method that requires both branch and SHA match
- Update done.go and mq_submit.go to resolve HEAD SHA and use SHA-based dedup
- Add supersede logic to done.go (close old MRs for same source issue)
- Add tests for commit_sha parsing and formatting

Closes GH#3032
